info.title: Dockerized distribution plan with CI, npm build, and docs

parts.text: To make it easier to use, this project could be distributed via a docker image. Create a plan to add a docker image, github action to build it, npm build action, and documentation for usage. The image should be tagged with distance from the last semver tag. Ask me any questions you need to understand this request better. Offer options and alternative if you have a better option for distribution (for example, can it be packaged as an app for osx/linux)?

<path>/home/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # PR Review Chatbot
2: 
3: A standalone web application that lets you review and ask questions about GitHub Pull Requests using an LLM. Built with React, TypeScript, and Vite.
4: 
5: ![PR Review Chatbot](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-18-blue) ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.7-blue) ![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-6-purple)
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Features
10: 
11: - 💬 **Chat interface** — Ask questions about a PR in natural language
12: - 🤖 **LLM integration** — Supports OpenAI v1 API and LiteLLM Proxy with streaming responses
13: - 🎭 **Demo mode** — Try the app immediately with a sample PR, no credentials needed
14: - 🐙 **GitHub integration** — Works with github.com and GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)
15: - 🔍 **PR context** — Injects PR metadata, file diffs, comments, and reviews into the LLM context
16: - 🧭 **Summary tab** — Auto-generates a PR kickoff summary in a dedicated tab (separate from chat)
17: - ⚙️ **Runtime configuration** — Override all settings via the Settings dialog (persisted to localStorage)
18: - 🌙 **Dark mode** — Toggle between light and dark themes
19: 
20: ## Active OpenSpec Change
21: 
22: The repository currently has an in-progress change: `add-pr-summary-tab`.
23: 
24: Planned summary behavior:
25: - Auto-generate a PR summary when a PR is selected (enabled by default)
26: - Show output in a dedicated `Summary` tab (separate from chat history/context)
27: - Favor quick orientation: a 2-4 line overview first
28: - Add adaptive `Focus Areas` only when warranted, with a hard cap of 4 and no minimum
29: - Treat summary output with no focus areas as valid for simple PRs
30: - Include commit messages as first-class summary context
31: - Reuse session cache per PR/prompt fingerprint and rate-limit generation to one request per minute per PR head SHA
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## Quick Start (Demo Mode)
36: 
37: The fastest way to try the app — no credentials required:
38: 
39: ```bash
40: # 1. Clone the repository
41: git clone <repo-url>
42: cd pr-review-chatbot
43: 
44: # 2. Install dependencies
45: npm install
46: 
47: # 3. Start the dev server
48: npm run dev
49: ```
50: 
51: Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) in your browser.
52: 
53: The app starts in **Demo Mode** with a pre-loaded sample PR (JWT authentication feature). You still need to configure an LLM API key to get responses — see [LLM Configuration](#llm-configuration) below.
54: 
55: ---
56: 
57: ## Configuration
58: 
59: Configuration can be set in two ways:
60: 
61: 1. **Environment file** (`.env`) — set defaults at build time
62: 2. **Settings dialog** — override at runtime (click the ⚙️ Settings button in the top bar)
63: 
64: Runtime settings are saved to `localStorage` and persist across page reloads.
65: 
66: ### Environment File
67: 
68: Copy the example file and fill in your values:
69: 
70: ```bash
71: cp .env.example .env
72: ```
73: 
74: Edit `.env`:
75: 
76: ```bash
77: # ─── GitHub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78: 
79: # Your GitHub Personal Access Token
80: # Required scopes: repo (for private repos) or public_repo (for public repos)
81: # Create one at: https://github.com/settings/tokens
82: VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
83: 
84: # GitHub instance
85: # Use "github.com" for GitHub.com
86: # Use your GHES hostname for GitHub Enterprise Server (e.g., github.mycompany.com)
87: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com
88: 
89: # ─── LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: 
91: # LLM backend type
92: # "openai"  → Direct OpenAI v1 API
93: # "litellm" → LiteLLM Proxy (same API format)
94: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai
95: 
96: # API endpoint base URL (without /chat/completions)
97: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
98: 
99: # Your LLM API key
100: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
101: 
102: # Model to use for chat completions
103: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
104: 
105: # ─── App ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
106: 
107: # Start in demo mode (true/false)
108: # Demo mode uses a pre-loaded sample PR — no GitHub credentials needed
109: VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
110: 
111: # Enable PR summary auto-generation by default (true/false)
112: VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
113: 
114: # Optional additional summary commands appended to summary prompt
115: VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
116: ```
117: 
118: ---
119: 
120: ## LLM Configuration
121: 
122: ### OpenAI
123: 
124: ```bash
125: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai
126: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
127: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
128: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
129: ```
130: 
131: ### LiteLLM Proxy
132: 
133: ```bash
134: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=litellm
135: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4000  # Your LiteLLM proxy URL
136: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=your-litellm-key        # Or leave empty if no auth
137: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o                   # Or any model your proxy supports
138: ```
139: 
140: ### Other OpenAI-compatible APIs
141: 
142: Any service that implements the OpenAI v1 API format works:
143: 
144: ```bash
145: # Azure OpenAI
146: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/<deployment>
147: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=your-azure-key
148: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
149: 
150: # Ollama (local)
151: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:11434/v1
152: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=ollama
153: VITE_LLM_MODEL=llama3.2
154: 
155: # Anthropic via LiteLLM
156: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4000
157: REDACTED
158: ```
159: 
160: ---
161: 
162: ## GitHub Configuration
163: 
164: ### GitHub.com
165: 
166: 1. Go to [GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens](https://github.com/settings/tokens)
167: 2. Create a new token (classic) with scopes:
168:    - `repo` — for private repositories
169:    - `public_repo` — for public repositories only
170: 3. Set in `.env`:
171: 
172: ```bash
173: VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
174: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com
175: ```
176: 
177: ### GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)
178: 
179: ```bash
180: VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
181: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.mycompany.com  # Your GHES hostname (no https://)
182: ```
183: 
184: The app automatically constructs the correct API URL:
185: - `github.com` → `https://api.github.com`
186: - `github.mycompany.com` → `https://github.mycompany.com/api/v3`
187: 
188: ---
189: 
190: ## Running the App
191: 
192: ### Development
193: 
194: ```bash
195: npm run dev
196: ```
197: 
198: Starts the dev server at [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) with hot module replacement.
199: 
200: ### Production Build
201: 
202: ```bash
203: npm run build
204: ```
205: 
206: Outputs to `dist/`. Serve with any static file server:
207: 
208: ```bash
209: # Preview the production build locally
210: npm run preview
211: 
212: # Or serve with npx serve
213: npx serve dist
214: 
215: # Or with Python
216: python3 -m http.server 8080 --directory dist
217: ```
218: 
219: ---
220: 
221: ## Using the App
222: 
223: ### Demo Mode (Default)
224: 
225: 1. Open the app — it starts in Demo Mode with a sample JWT authentication PR
226: 2. Configure your LLM API key in **Settings** (⚙️ button in the top bar)
227: 3. Type a question in the chat input and press **Enter**
228: 
229: Example questions to try:
230: - *"Summarize this PR"*
231: - *"What are the security implications of this change?"*
232: - *"Are there any potential bugs in the login function?"*
233: - *"Explain the JWT token refresh flow"*
234: - *"What tests are missing?"*
235: 
236: ### GitHub Mode
237: 
238: 1. Disable Demo Mode in the sidebar or Settings
239: 2. Configure your GitHub PAT and instance in Settings
240: 3. In the sidebar, enter a repository as `owner/repo` (for example, `octocat/Hello-World`)
241: 4. Enter the PR number and click **Load PR**
242: 5. Use the refresh button in the Pull Requests header to reload the currently selected PR
243: 
244: Summary behavior in GitHub mode:
245: - A summary request is triggered after PR metadata/files/comments/reviews/commits load
246: - Summary output is rendered in the `Summary` tab and is not inserted into chat history/context
247: - Empty textual diffs skip LLM generation and show `Nothing to Summarize`
248: - LLM failures show `Unable to generate summary`
249: - Cache is session-scoped and keyed by PR identity + head SHA + prompt/commands fingerprint
250: - Generation is limited to one request per minute for each PR head SHA
251: 
252: GitHub mode behavior:
253: - While data is loading, the **Load PR** button shows a spinner and inputs are disabled to prevent duplicate requests.
254: - Auth failures (401) prompt you to update your GitHub PAT in Settings.
255: - Not found errors (404) tell you to verify `owner/repo` and PR number.
256: - Rate limit errors (403/429) show a retry message so you can wait and refresh.
257: - Refresh re-fetches metadata, files, comments, review comments, and reviews for the selected PR.
258: 
259: ### Settings Dialog
260: 
261: Click the **⚙️ Settings** button in the top bar to configure:
262: 
263: | Setting | Description |
264: |---------|-------------|
265: | Demo Mode | Toggle between demo data and real GitHub data |
266: | GitHub PAT | Personal Access Token for GitHub API |
267: | GitHub Instance | `github.com` or your GHES hostname |
268: | LLM Backend | `openai` or `litellm` |
269: | LLM Endpoint | Base URL for the LLM API |
270: | LLM API Key | Your API key |
271: | LLM Model | Model name (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `claude-3-5-sonnet`) |
272: | Summary Enabled | Toggle auto-generation for PR summaries |
273: | Summary Prompt | Editable base prompt for summary generation |
274: | Additional Summary Commands | Optional appended instructions for summaries |
275: 
276: ---
277: 
278: ## Architecture
279: 
280: ```mermaid
281: graph TD
282:     subgraph UI[PR Review Chatbot]
283:         SB[Sidebar\n- Demo mode toggle\n- PR list]
284:         CW[Chat Window\n- Message history\n- Streaming responses\n- Markdown + code highlighting]
285:         PV[PR Viewer\n- PR metadata\n- File diffs\n- Comments / Reviews]
286:     end
287: 
288:     subgraph Services[Services]
289:         LLM[LLM Service\n- OpenAI v1 API\n- LiteLLM Proxy\n- Streaming SSE\n- PR context injection]
290:         GH[GitHub Service\n- github.com\n- GHES support\n- PAT auth]
291:     end
292: 
293:     subgraph Store[Redux Store]
294:         CS[configSlice]
295:         CH[chatSlice]
296:         PS[prsSlice]
297:     end
298: 
299:     CW -->|sends messages| LLM
300:     SB -->|selects PR| GH
301:     GH -->|PR data| PS
302:     LLM -->|streamed reply| CH
303:     CS -->|config| LLM
304:     CS -->|config| GH
305:     PS -->|PR context| CW
306:     PS -->|PR context| PV
307:     CH -->|messages| CW
308: ```
309: 
310: ---
311: 
312: ## Troubleshooting
313: 
314: ### "Configure LLM API key in Settings" warning
315: You need to set an LLM API key. Click ⚙️ Settings and fill in the **API Key** and **Endpoint** fields.
316: 
317: ### LLM returns an error
318: - Check that your API key is correct
319: - Verify the endpoint URL (should not end with `/chat/completions`)
320: - Ensure the model name is correct for your provider
321: - Check browser console for detailed error messages
322: 
323: ### Summary output expectations
324: - Successful summaries should start with a concise 2-4 line orientation section
325: - `Focus Areas` are adaptive and optional for simple PRs
326: - When present, `Focus Areas` should remain within 1-4 items and include where/why/what-to-verify guidance
327: 
328: ### GitHub API errors
329: - Verify your PAT has the correct scopes (`repo` or `public_repo`)
330: - For GHES, ensure the hostname is correct (no `https://` prefix)
331: - Check that your PAT hasn't expired
332: - If you get a 404, confirm the `owner/repo` and PR number are correct
333: - If you hit rate limits, wait briefly, then use the sidebar refresh button
334: 
335: ### CORS errors with LiteLLM
336: If you see CORS errors when using a local LiteLLM proxy, add CORS headers to your proxy configuration or use a browser extension to disable CORS for development.
337: 
338: ---
339: 
340: ## License
341: 
342: MIT

(End of file - total 342 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# PR Review Chatbot

A standalone web application that lets you review and ask questions about GitHub Pull Requests using an LLM. Built with React, TypeScript, and Vite.

![PR Review Chatbot](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-18-blue) ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.7-blue) ![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-6-purple)

---

## Features

- 💬 **Chat interface** — Ask questions about a PR in natural language
- 🤖 **LLM integration** — Supports OpenAI v1 API and LiteLLM Proxy with streaming responses
- 🎭 **Demo mode** — Try the app immediately with a sample PR, no credentials needed
- 🐙 **GitHub integration** — Works with github.com and GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)
- 🔍 **PR context** — Injects PR metadata, file diffs, comments, and reviews into the LLM context
- 🧭 **Summary tab** — Auto-generates a PR kickoff summary in a dedicated tab (separate from chat)
- ⚙️ **Runtime configuration** — Override all settings via the Settings dialog (persisted to localStorage)
- 🌙 **Dark mode** — Toggle between light and dark themes

## Active OpenSpec Change

    types: [created]

    types: [created]

jobs:
  opencode:
    if: |
      contains(github.event.comment.body, ' /oc') ||
      startsWith(github.event.comment.body, '/oc') ||
      contains(github.event.comment.body, ' /opencode') ||
      startsWith(github.event.comment.body, '/opencode')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read

2: 
3: ## Project Overview
4: 
5: This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.
6: 
7: ## Tech Stack
8: 
9: - **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
10: - **Redux Toolkit** for state management
11: - **Tailwind CSS** for styling
12: - **Radix UI** for accessible components
13: - **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
14: - **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering
15: 
16: ## Project Structure
17: 
18: ```
19: src/
20: ├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout
21: ├── main.tsx                   # Entry point with Redux Provider
22: ├── index.css                  # Tailwind + CSS variables
23: ├── vite-env.d.ts              # Vite environment type declarations
24: ├── types/
25: │   └── index.ts               # All TypeScript interfaces
26: ├── store/
27: │   ├── index.ts               # Redux store configuration
28: │   ├── hooks.ts               # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
29: │   └── slices/
30: │       ├── configSlice.ts     # App config (env vars + localStorage overrides)
31: │       ├── chatSlice.ts       # Chat messages + streaming state
32: │       ├── prsSlice.ts        # Selected PR data + repo PR lists + commits + summary lifecycle state
33: │       └── watchedReposSlice.ts # Watched repositories + PR count badges (localStorage)
34: ├── services/
35: │   ├── llm.ts                 # LLM service (OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM, streaming)
36: │   ├── github.ts              # GitHub API service (github.com + GHES)
37: │   ├── summary.ts             # Summary cache/rate-limit/empty-diff utilities
38: │   └── dummyData.ts           # Sample PR data for demo mode
39: ├── components/
40: │   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # Demo toggle + watchlist + controls/list sidebar views
41: │   ├── PRList.tsx             # Sidebar repository PR list view with back navigation
42: │   ├── ChatWindow.tsx         # Chat interface with streaming
43: │   ├── PRViewer.tsx           # PR details, summary tab, diffs, comments, reviews
44: │   └── SettingsDialog.tsx     # Runtime configuration override + summary controls
45: └── lib/
46:     └── utils.ts               # cn(), formatDate(), generateId()
47: ```
48: 
49: ## Key Features
50: 
51: ### Demo Mode (Default)
52: - Pre-loaded with a sample JWT authentication PR
53: - No GitHub credentials required
54: - Toggle in sidebar or Settings dialog
55: - Allows immediate LLM interaction
56: 
57: ### LLM Integration
58: - Supports **OpenAI v1 API** and **LiteLLM Proxy** (same API format)
59: - Streaming responses via Server-Sent Events
60: - PR context injected as system prompt (PR metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
61: - Configurable endpoint, model, and API key
62: - Separate summary prompt assembly path (`buildSummaryPrompt`) for orientation-first PR kickoff summaries
63: 
64: ### GitHub Integration
65: - PAT (Personal Access Token) authentication
66: - Supports **github.com** and **GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)**
67: - GHES base URL: `https://<host>/api/v3`
68: - Manual GitHub mode input in sidebar: `owner/repo` + optional PR number
69: - Sidebar supports `Load PR` (single selected list item) and `Load All PRs` (repository list)
70: - Watch repositories in sidebar and display open PR count badges
71: - Fetches: PR metadata, file diffs, issue comments, review comments, reviews, commit messages
72: - Refresh actions re-fetch currently selected PR, repository lists, and watched repo PR counts
73: - User-friendly GitHub API errors (auth, not found, rate limit, network)
74: 
75: ### Settings
76: - Runtime override of all configuration
77: - Persisted to `localStorage`
78: - Defaults from environment variables (`VITE_*`)
79: - Includes summary controls (`summaryEnabled`, editable `summaryPrompt`, optional `summaryCommands`, reset prompt)
80: 
81: ### PR Summary Flow
82: - Summary generation triggers on PR selection/load when `summaryEnabled` is true
83: - Summary state is isolated from chat state (`prsSlice.summary`)
84: - Fallback states:
85:   - `Nothing to Summarize` when no textual diffs exist
86:   - `Unable to generate summary` on generation failure
87: - Uses session-scoped cache and one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limit
88: - Summary output contract:
89:   - 2-4 line orientation section
90:   - adaptive `Focus Areas` (`0..4`)
91:   - each focus area includes where/why/what-to-verify
92: 
93: ## Environment Variables
94: 
95: ```bash
96: VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
97: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
98: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com  # "github.com" or GHES hostname
99: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai   # "openai" or "litellm"
100: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
101: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
102: VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
103: VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
104: VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
105: ```
106: 
107: ## Development
108: 
109: ```bash
110: npm install       # Install dependencies
111: npm run dev       # Start dev server (http://localhost:5173)
112: npm run lint      # Run ESLint (flat config in eslint.config.js)
113: npm run build     # TypeScript check + production build
114: npm run preview   # Preview production build
115: ```
116: 
117: ## Common Tasks for AI Agents
118: 
119: ### Adding a new Redux slice
120: 1. Create `src/store/slices/<name>Slice.ts` following the pattern in existing slices
121: 2. Add the reducer to `src/store/index.ts`
122: 3. Export typed selectors using `useAppSelector`
123: 
124: ### Adding a new component
125: 1. Create `src/components/<Name>.tsx`
126: 2. Use `useAppSelector` and `useAppDispatch` from `src/store/hooks.ts`
127: 3. Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional class names
128: 4. Follow Tailwind CSS + CSS variable pattern for theming
129: 
130: ### Modifying the LLM system prompt
131: - Edit `buildSystemPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
132: - The method receives a `PRContext` object with `pr`, `files`, `comments`, `reviewComments`, `reviews`
133: - Prompt construction uses a deterministic character budget for very large PRs and emits an omission summary when some diffs are excluded
134: 
135: ### Modifying summary behavior
136: - Summary orchestration policy lives in [`src/services/summary.ts`](src/services/summary.ts)
137: - Summary state transitions and async generation thunk live in [`src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`](src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts)
138: - Summary prompt contract lives in `buildSummaryPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
139: - Summary UI rendering lives in [`src/components/PRViewer.tsx`](src/components/PRViewer.tsx)
140: 
141: ### Adding GitHub API endpoints
142: - Add methods to the `GitHubService` class in [`src/services/github.ts`](src/services/github.ts)
143: - The service automatically handles github.com vs GHES URL routing
144: - Prefer using the centralized GitHub error parsing (`parseGitHubError`) and `GitHubApiError` for consistent UI-friendly failures
145: 
146: ### GitHub data loading flow
147: - `prsSlice` includes async thunks for PR metadata and per-resource loading:
148:   - `fetchPullRequest`
149:   - `fetchPRFiles`
150:   - `fetchPRComments`
151:   - `fetchPRReviewComments`
152:   - `fetchPRReviews`
153: - Use `loadingByResource` and `errorByResource` for UI state; legacy aggregate `isLoading` and `error` are still populated for compatibility
154: 
155: ### Active OpenSpec context
156: - Active change: `openspec/changes/repository-watcher`
157: - Main specs synced for this change:
158:   - `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`
159:   - `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`
160: - Repository watcher behavior target:
161:   - left `PR Review` pane supports two views: controls/watchlist and repo PR list
162:   - `Load PR` with a PR number switches to list view with one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
163:   - watched repo click opens that repo list in the same pane
164:   - list view includes Back action to return to controls/watchlist
165: 
166: ### Modifying dummy data
167: - Edit [`src/services/dummyData.ts`](src/services/dummyData.ts)
168: - The `dummyPRContext` export is used by `ChatWindow` and `Sidebar` in demo mode
169: 
170: ## Architecture Notes
171: 
172: ### State Flow
173: ```
174: User types message
175:   → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (user)
176:   → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (assistant, isStreaming: true)
177:   → LLMService.chatStream() yields chunks
178:   → ChatWindow dispatches appendStreamingContent for each chunk
179:   → ChatWindow dispatches finalizeStreamingMessage when done
180: ```
181: 
182: ### Config Priority
183: ```
184: localStorage override > environment variable > hardcoded default
185: ```
186: 
187: ### GHES URL Construction
188: ```typescript
189: // github.com → https://api.github.com
190: // mycompany.com → https://mycompany.com/api/v3
191: ```
192: 
193: ## Known Limitations / Future Work
194: 
195: - No pagination for PR lists
196: - No OAuth flow (PAT only)
197: - No conversation persistence across page reloads

(End of file - total 197 lines)
</content>

# PR Review Chatbot - Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.

## Tech Stack

- **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
- **Redux Toolkit** for state management
- **Tailwind CSS** for styling
- **Radix UI** for accessible components
- **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
- **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering

## Project Structure

src/
├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout

parts.text:
Great request — based on your choices, here’s the implementation plan I’d execute.

**Plan**
- Add container packaging for this Vite app with a multi-stage `Dockerfile` (Node build -> Nginx runtime) plus `.dockerignore`; image serves `dist/` and exposes port `80`.
- Add deterministic image tagging logic based on semver distance: compute latest semver tag (`vX.Y.Z`), count commits since it, and emit `vX.Y.Z-N` (your preferred format); also add a `sha-<shortsha>` tag for traceability.
- Add a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow for Docker publish to GHCR, triggered on release publish (per your preference), using `actions/checkout` with full history (`fetch-depth: 0`), `docker/setup-buildx-action`, `docker/login-action`, and `docker/build-push-action`.
- Add/adjust npm build CI workflow so `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` run in CI; keep this separate from Docker publish so app quality gates are clear.
- Update docs in `README.md` with: local Docker build/run, GHCR pull/run commands, explanation of `vX.Y.Z-N` tag semantics, and note that this frontend is static (runtime config is via Settings/localStorage unless baked in at image build time).
- Add a short release section documenting that release builds publish Docker images and how to find tags in GHCR.

**Concrete file changes (planned)**
- `Dockerfile`
- `.dockerignore`
- `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml` (new)
- `.github/workflows/build.yml` (or split into `ci.yml` for npm build/test/lint)
- `package.json` scripts (`docker:build`, `docker:run`, optional `docker:tag:distance` helper)
- `README.md`
- `plans/plan.md` and `agents.md` updated to reflect the new distribution workflow

**Tagging behavior**
- Semver base: last reachable tag matching `^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`
- Distance: `git rev-list <last-semver>..HEAD --count`
- Output tag: `vX.Y.Z-N` (example: `v1.3.0-12`)
- Fallback if no semver tag exists: `v0.0.0-<commit-count>` (or fail fast; see question below)

**Alternative distribution (Tauri, as requested)**
- Good fit for this project because it’s a static React frontend and Tauri gives native installers for macOS/Linux with smaller footprint than Electron.
- If we add it later, plan would be: scaffold Tauri wrapper, point to Vite build output/dev server, add release workflow for signed artifacts, document install/update flow.
- I’d treat Docker + GHCR as immediate distribution, Tauri as phase 2.

One targeted question before implementation:
- Do you want the Docker publish workflow to **fail** when no semver tag exists yet, or use fallback `v0.0.0-N` (recommended)?  
  - Recommended default: `v0.0.0-N` so first releases still publish cleanly.  
  - If you choose fail-fast, I’ll add explicit validation and a clear error message instructing maintainers to create the first semver tag.
Use fall back of v0.0.0-N. There is a v1.0.0 tag on this repo

Perfect — we’ll use fallback `v0.0.0-N`, and since this repo already has `v1.0.0`, normal tagging will resolve to `v1.0.0-N` until next semver tag.

**Implementation Plan**
- Add container packaging:
  - Create multi-stage `Dockerfile` (Node build stage, Nginx runtime stage) to serve `dist/`.
  - Add `.dockerignore` to keep build context small.
  - Expose `80`, include a simple healthcheck endpoint/path.
- Add Docker tag computation:
  - In workflow, fetch full git history/tags.
  - Detect latest semver tag matching `vX.Y.Z`.
  - Compute distance `N = git rev-list <tag>..HEAD --count`.
  - Emit primary tag `vX.Y.Z-N`; fallback to `v0.0.0-N` if no semver tag exists.
  - Also emit `sha-<shortsha>` as a secondary trace tag.
- Add GitHub Action to publish image to GHCR on release:
  - New workflow (e.g. `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml`) triggered on `release.published`.
  - Login to GHCR using `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
  - Build/push image via Buildx to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>`.
  - Attach OCI labels (source, revision, created date, version).
- Add npm build/quality workflow (CI):
  - Ensure CI runs `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run build`.
  - Keep this independent from Docker publish so failures are obvious and fast.
- Update docs:
  - Add Docker usage (`docker pull/run`) and local build instructions.
  - Document tag meaning (`vX.Y.Z-N`) and fallback behavior.
  - Add release flow docs: “publishing a GitHub Release pushes a GHCR image”.
- Update project planning/agent docs:
  - Reflect new workflows and distribution approach in `plans/plan.md` and `agents.md`.

**Validation Plan**
- Verify tag script locally against `v1.0.0` and a no-semver simulation.
- Validate Docker image boots and serves app at `http://localhost`.
- Confirm CI passes (`lint`, `test`, `build`).
- Confirm release workflow computes expected tags and pushes to GHCR.

**Alternative Distribution (Recommended phase 2)**
- Tauri desktop packaging for macOS/Linux:
  - Keep Docker for server/container users.
  - Add Tauri for installable desktop app distribution.
  - Separate release workflow can publish signed desktop artifacts later.

If you want, next step I can execute this exact plan and implement all files/workflows/docs in one pass.
Execute the plan. Review the code before finishing each stage. Ensure tests pass. Commit when complete.

Got it. I’m ready to execute, but I’m currently under a **read-only plan-mode constraint**, so I cannot edit files, run write operations, or commit yet.

Here is the exact staged execution plan I’ll run immediately once you switch out of plan mode.

**Stage 1: Docker Image Packaging**
- Add `Dockerfile` (multi-stage):
  - builder: `node:20-alpine`, `npm ci`, `npm run build`
  - runtime: `nginx:alpine`, copy `dist/`, expose `80`
- Add `.dockerignore` (`node_modules`, `dist`, `.git`, logs, env files)
- Optional `nginx.conf` only if SPA fallback is needed (`try_files ... /index.html`)
- Add npm helper scripts in `package.json`:
  - `docker:build`
  - `docker:run`

Review checkpoint before moving on:
- Verify Dockerfile uses deterministic install (`npm ci`) and smallest runtime image.
- Verify no secrets copied into image layers.
- Verify app loads from container locally.

Validation:
- `npm run lint && npm test && npm run build`
- `docker build -t pr-review-chatbot:local .`
- `docker run --rm -p 8080:80 pr-review-chatbot:local`

**Stage 2: GHCR Release Workflow + Semver-Distance Tagging**
- Add `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml` triggered on `release.published`
- Use full git history (`fetch-depth: 0`)
- Compute tags:
  - latest semver `vX.Y.Z` if present
  - distance `N = commits since tag`
  - primary tag: `vX.Y.Z-N`
  - fallback: `v0.0.0-N`
  - secondary trace tag: `sha-<shortsha>`
- Build and push to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>` using Buildx and `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Add OCI labels (source, revision, created)

Review checkpoint:
- Verify regex only matches strict semver tags.
- Verify fallback behavior works when semver tag absent.
- Verify permissions are minimal (`contents: read`, `packages: write`).

Validation:
- Lint workflow syntax
- Dry-run tag script locally with current repo (should resolve from existing `v1.0.0`)

**Stage 3: NPM Build Action (CI)**
- Update CI workflow (or add `ci.yml`) to include:
  - `npm ci`
  - `npm run lint`
  - `npm test`
  - `npm run build`
- Keep CI independent from release Docker publish

Review checkpoint:
- Ensure CI remains fast and deterministic.
- Ensure no duplicate or conflicting workflow triggers.

Validation:
- Run same sequence locally before finalizing.

**Stage 4: Documentation**
- Update `README.md` with:
  - Pull/run GHCR image
  - Local docker build/run
  - Tag semantics (`vX.Y.Z-N`, fallback `v0.0.0-N`)
  - Release behavior (Docker publish happens on GitHub Release publish)
- Add brief “Distribution alternatives” section:
  - Docker (primary)
  - Tauri desktop packaging (phase 2 recommendation)

Review checkpoint:
- Confirm docs reflect actual workflow names and commands exactly.
- Confirm GHCR path examples use your repo naming.

**Stage 5: Repo Meta + Final Verification + Commit**
- Update `plans/plan.md` and `agents.md` to reflect new workflows/distribution
- Run full gate:
  - `npm run lint && npm test && npm run build`
- If all pass, commit with message like:
  - `Add Docker distribution and release publish workflow`

---

Once plan mode is disabled, I’ll execute all of this end-to-end, review after each stage, run tests, and create the commit.

  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Type-check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Lint Test Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

name: Docker Release

on:
  release:
    types:
      - published

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Build and Publish Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Compute Docker tags
        id: version
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          image="ghcr.io/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY,,}"
          last_semver_tag="$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --match 'v[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*' 2>/dev/null || true)"

          if [ -n "$last_semver_tag" ]; then
            distance="$(git rev-list "${last_semver_tag}..HEAD" --count)"
            base_tag="$last_semver_tag"
          else
            distance="$(git rev-list HEAD --count)"
            base_tag="v0.0.0"
          fi

          version_tag="${base_tag}-${distance}"
          sha_tag="sha-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"

          echo "image=$image" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_tag=$version_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "sha_tag=$sha_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Build and push image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          file: ./Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: |
            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}
            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.sha_tag }}
          labels: |
            org.opencontainers.image.source=${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}
            org.opencontainers.image.revision=${{ github.sha }}
            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:1.27-alpine AS runtime

COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

EXPOSE 80

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1

# PR Review Chatbot

A standalone web application that lets you review and ask questions about GitHub Pull Requests using an LLM. Built with React, TypeScript, and Vite.

![PR Review Chatbot](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-18-blue) ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.7-blue) ![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-6-purple)

---

## Features

- 💬 **Chat interface** — Ask questions about a PR in natural language
- 🤖 **LLM integration** — Supports OpenAI v1 API and LiteLLM Proxy with streaming responses
- 🎭 **Demo mode** — Try the app immediately with a sample PR, no credentials needed
- 🐙 **GitHub integration** — Works with github.com and GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)
- 🔍 **PR context** — Injects PR metadata, file diffs, comments, and reviews into the LLM context
- 🧭 **Summary tab** — Auto-generates a PR kickoff summary in a dedicated tab (separate from chat)
- ⚙️ **Runtime configuration** — Override all settings via the Settings dialog (persisted to localStorage)
- 🌙 **Dark mode** — Toggle between light and dark themes

## Active OpenSpec Change

The repository currently has an in-progress change: `add-pr-summary-tab`.

Planned summary behavior:
- Auto-generate a PR summary when a PR is selected (enabled by default)
- Show output in a dedicated `Summary` tab (separate from chat history/context)
- Favor quick orientation: a 2-4 line overview first
- Add adaptive `Focus Areas` only when warranted, with a hard cap of 4 and no minimum
- Treat summary output with no focus areas as valid for simple PRs
- Include commit messages as first-class summary context
- Reuse session cache per PR/prompt fingerprint and rate-limit generation to one request per minute per PR head SHA

---

## Quick Start (Demo Mode)

The fastest way to try the app — no credentials required:

```bash
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone <repo-url>
cd pr-review-chatbot

# 2. Install dependencies
npm install

# 3. Start the dev server
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) in your browser.

The app starts in **Demo Mode** with a pre-loaded sample PR (JWT authentication feature). You still need to configure an LLM API key to get responses — see [LLM Configuration](#llm-configuration) below.

---

## Configuration

Configuration can be set in two ways:

1. **Environment file** (`.env`) — set defaults at build time
2. **Settings dialog** — override at runtime (click the ⚙️ Settings button in the top bar)

Runtime settings are saved to `localStorage` and persist across page reloads.

### Environment File

Copy the example file and fill in your values:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env`:

```bash
# ─── GitHub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Your GitHub Personal Access Token
# Required scopes: repo (for private repos) or public_repo (for public repos)
# Create one at: https://github.com/settings/tokens
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# GitHub instance
# Use "github.com" for GitHub.com
# Use your GHES hostname for GitHub Enterprise Server (e.g., github.mycompany.com)
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com

# ─── LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# LLM backend type
# "openai"  → Direct OpenAI v1 API
# "litellm" → LiteLLM Proxy (same API format)
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai

# API endpoint base URL (without /chat/completions)
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1

# Your LLM API key
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# Model to use for chat completions
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o

# ─── App ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Start in demo mode (true/false)
# Demo mode uses a pre-loaded sample PR — no GitHub credentials needed
VITE_DEMO_MODE=true

# Enable PR summary auto-generation by default (true/false)
VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true

# Optional additional summary commands appended to summary prompt
VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
```

---

## LLM Configuration

### OpenAI

```bash
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
```

### LiteLLM Proxy

```bash
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=litellm
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4000  # Your LiteLLM proxy URL
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=your-litellm-key        # Or leave empty if no auth
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o                   # Or any model your proxy supports
```

### Other OpenAI-compatible APIs

Any service that implements the OpenAI v1 API format works:

```bash
# Azure OpenAI
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/<deployment>
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=your-azure-key
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o

# Ollama (local)
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:11434/v1
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=ollama
VITE_LLM_MODEL=llama3.2

# Anthropic via LiteLLM
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4000
REDACTED
```

---

## GitHub Configuration

### GitHub.com

1. Go to [GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens](https://github.com/settings/tokens)
2. Create a new token (classic) with scopes:
   - `repo` — for private repositories
   - `public_repo` — for public repositories only
3. Set in `.env`:

```bash
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com
```

### GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)

```bash
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.mycompany.com  # Your GHES hostname (no https://)
```

The app automatically constructs the correct API URL:
- `github.com` → `https://api.github.com`
- `github.mycompany.com` → `https://github.mycompany.com/api/v3`

---

## Running the App

### Development

```bash
npm run dev
```

Starts the dev server at [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173) with hot module replacement.

### Production Build

```bash
npm run build
```

Outputs to `dist/`. Serve with any static file server:

```bash
# Preview the production build locally
npm run preview

# Or serve with npx serve
npx serve dist

# Or with Python
python3 -m http.server 8080 --directory dist
```

---

## Using the App

### Demo Mode (Default)

1. Open the app — it starts in Demo Mode with a sample JWT authentication PR
2. Configure your LLM API key in **Settings** (⚙️ button in the top bar)
3. Type a question in the chat input and press **Enter**

Example questions to try:
- *"Summarize this PR"*
- *"What are the security implications of this change?"*
- *"Are there any potential bugs in the login function?"*
- *"Explain the JWT token refresh flow"*
- *"What tests are missing?"*

### GitHub Mode

1. Disable Demo Mode in the sidebar or Settings
2. Configure your GitHub PAT and instance in Settings
3. In the sidebar, enter a repository as `owner/repo` (for example, `octocat/Hello-World`)
4. Enter the PR number and click **Load PR**
5. Use the refresh button in the Pull Requests header to reload the currently selected PR

Summary behavior in GitHub mode:
- A summary request is triggered after PR metadata/files/comments/reviews/commits load
- Summary output is rendered in the `Summary` tab and is not inserted into chat history/context
- Empty textual diffs skip LLM generation and show `Nothing to Summarize`
- LLM failures show `Unable to generate summary`
- Cache is session-scoped and keyed by PR identity + head SHA + prompt/commands fingerprint
- Generation is limited to one request per minute for each PR head SHA

GitHub mode behavior:
- While data is loading, the **Load PR** button shows a spinner and inputs are disabled to prevent duplicate requests.
- Auth failures (401) prompt you to update your GitHub PAT in Settings.
- Not found errors (404) tell you to verify `owner/repo` and PR number.
- Rate limit errors (403/429) show a retry message so you can wait and refresh.
- Refresh re-fetches metadata, files, comments, review comments, and reviews for the selected PR.

### Settings Dialog

Click the **⚙️ Settings** button in the top bar to configure:

| Setting | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Demo Mode | Toggle between demo data and real GitHub data |
| GitHub PAT | Personal Access Token for GitHub API |
| GitHub Instance | `github.com` or your GHES hostname |
| LLM Backend | `openai` or `litellm` |
| LLM Endpoint | Base URL for the LLM API |
| LLM API Key | Your API key |
| LLM Model | Model name (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `claude-3-5-sonnet`) |
| Summary Enabled | Toggle auto-generation for PR summaries |
| Summary Prompt | Editable base prompt for summary generation |
| Additional Summary Commands | Optional appended instructions for summaries |

---

## Architecture

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph UI[PR Review Chatbot]
        SB[Sidebar\n- Demo mode toggle\n- PR list]
        CW[Chat Window\n- Message history\n- Streaming responses\n- Markdown + code highlighting]
        PV[PR Viewer\n- PR metadata\n- File diffs\n- Comments / Reviews]
    end

    subgraph Services[Services]
        LLM[LLM Service\n- OpenAI v1 API\n- LiteLLM Proxy\n- Streaming SSE\n- PR context injection]
        GH[GitHub Service\n- github.com\n- GHES support\n- PAT auth]
    end

    subgraph Store[Redux Store]
        CS[configSlice]
        CH[chatSlice]
        PS[prsSlice]
    end

    CW -->|sends messages| LLM
    SB -->|selects PR| GH
    GH -->|PR data| PS
    LLM -->|streamed reply| CH
    CS -->|config| LLM
    CS -->|config| GH
    PS -->|PR context| CW
    PS -->|PR context| PV
    CH -->|messages| CW
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Configure LLM API key in Settings" warning
You need to set an LLM API key. Click ⚙️ Settings and fill in the **API Key** and **Endpoint** fields.

### LLM returns an error
- Check that your API key is correct
- Verify the endpoint URL (should not end with `/chat/completions`)
- Ensure the model name is correct for your provider
- Check browser console for detailed error messages

### Summary output expectations
- Successful summaries should start with a concise 2-4 line orientation section
- `Focus Areas` are adaptive and optional for simple PRs
- When present, `Focus Areas` should remain within 1-4 items and include where/why/what-to-verify guidance

### GitHub API errors
- Verify your PAT has the correct scopes (`repo` or `public_repo`)
- For GHES, ensure the hostname is correct (no `https://` prefix)
- Check that your PAT hasn't expired
- If you get a 404, confirm the `owner/repo` and PR number are correct
- If you hit rate limits, wait briefly, then use the sidebar refresh button

### CORS errors with LiteLLM
If you see CORS errors when using a local LiteLLM proxy, add CORS headers to your proxy configuration or use a browser extension to disable CORS for development.

---

## License

MIT

If you see CORS errors when using a local LiteLLM proxy, add CORS headers to your proxy configuration or use a browser extension to disable CORS for development.

---

## Distribution Options

- **Docker + GHCR (current):** Best for consistent cross-platform runs in CI, servers, and developer machines.
- **Tauri desktop app (alternative):** Good next step if you want native installers for macOS/Linux/Windows with a smaller runtime footprint than Electron.

---

## License

MIT

# PR Review Chatbot - Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.

## Tech Stack

- **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
- **Redux Toolkit** for state management
- **Tailwind CSS** for styling
- **Radix UI** for accessible components
- **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
- **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout
├── main.tsx                   # Entry point with Redux Provider
├── index.css                  # Tailwind + CSS variables
├── vite-env.d.ts              # Vite environment type declarations
├── types/
│   └── index.ts               # All TypeScript interfaces
├── store/
│   ├── index.ts               # Redux store configuration
│   ├── hooks.ts               # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
│   └── slices/
│       ├── configSlice.ts     # App config (env vars + localStorage overrides)
│       ├── chatSlice.ts       # Chat messages + streaming state
│       ├── prsSlice.ts        # Selected PR data + repo PR lists + commits + summary lifecycle state
│       └── watchedReposSlice.ts # Watched repositories + PR count badges (localStorage)
├── services/
│   ├── llm.ts                 # LLM service (OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM, streaming)
│   ├── github.ts              # GitHub API service (github.com + GHES)
│   ├── summary.ts             # Summary cache/rate-limit/empty-diff utilities
│   └── dummyData.ts           # Sample PR data for demo mode
├── components/
│   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # Demo toggle + watchlist + controls/list sidebar views
│   ├── PRList.tsx             # Sidebar repository PR list view with back navigation
│   ├── ChatWindow.tsx         # Chat interface with streaming
│   ├── PRViewer.tsx           # PR details, summary tab, diffs, comments, reviews
│   └── SettingsDialog.tsx     # Runtime configuration override + summary controls
└── lib/
    └── utils.ts               # cn(), formatDate(), generateId()
```

## Key Features

### Demo Mode (Default)
- Pre-loaded with a sample JWT authentication PR
- No GitHub credentials required
- Toggle in sidebar or Settings dialog
- Allows immediate LLM interaction

### LLM Integration
- Supports **OpenAI v1 API** and **LiteLLM Proxy** (same API format)
- Streaming responses via Server-Sent Events
- PR context injected as system prompt (PR metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
- Configurable endpoint, model, and API key
- Separate summary prompt assembly path (`buildSummaryPrompt`) for orientation-first PR kickoff summaries

### GitHub Integration
- PAT (Personal Access Token) authentication
- Supports **github.com** and **GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)**
- GHES base URL: `https://<host>/api/v3`
- Manual GitHub mode input in sidebar: `owner/repo` + optional PR number
- Sidebar supports `Load PR` (single selected list item) and `Load All PRs` (repository list)
- Watch repositories in sidebar and display open PR count badges
- Fetches: PR metadata, file diffs, issue comments, review comments, reviews, commit messages
- Refresh actions re-fetch currently selected PR, repository lists, and watched repo PR counts
- User-friendly GitHub API errors (auth, not found, rate limit, network)

### Settings
- Runtime override of all configuration
- Persisted to `localStorage`
- Defaults from environment variables (`VITE_*`)
- Includes summary controls (`summaryEnabled`, editable `summaryPrompt`, optional `summaryCommands`, reset prompt)

### PR Summary Flow
- Summary generation triggers on PR selection/load when `summaryEnabled` is true
- Summary state is isolated from chat state (`prsSlice.summary`)
- Fallback states:
  - `Nothing to Summarize` when no textual diffs exist
  - `Unable to generate summary` on generation failure
- Uses session-scoped cache and one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limit
- Summary output contract:
  - 2-4 line orientation section
  - adaptive `Focus Areas` (`0..4`)
  - each focus area includes where/why/what-to-verify

## Environment Variables

```bash
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com  # "github.com" or GHES hostname
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai   # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
```

## Development

```bash
npm install       # Install dependencies
npm run dev       # Start dev server (http://localhost:5173)
npm run lint      # Run ESLint (flat config in eslint.config.js)
npm run build     # TypeScript check + production build
npm run preview   # Preview production build
```

## Common Tasks for AI Agents

### Adding a new Redux slice
1. Create `src/store/slices/<name>Slice.ts` following the pattern in existing slices
2. Add the reducer to `src/store/index.ts`
3. Export typed selectors using `useAppSelector`

### Adding a new component
1. Create `src/components/<Name>.tsx`
2. Use `useAppSelector` and `useAppDispatch` from `src/store/hooks.ts`
3. Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional class names
4. Follow Tailwind CSS + CSS variable pattern for theming

### Modifying the LLM system prompt
- Edit `buildSystemPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- The method receives a `PRContext` object with `pr`, `files`, `comments`, `reviewComments`, `reviews`
- Prompt construction uses a deterministic character budget for very large PRs and emits an omission summary when some diffs are excluded

### Modifying summary behavior
- Summary orchestration policy lives in [`src/services/summary.ts`](src/services/summary.ts)
- Summary state transitions and async generation thunk live in [`src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`](src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts)
- Summary prompt contract lives in `buildSummaryPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- Summary UI rendering lives in [`src/components/PRViewer.tsx`](src/components/PRViewer.tsx)

### Adding GitHub API endpoints
- Add methods to the `GitHubService` class in [`src/services/github.ts`](src/services/github.ts)
- The service automatically handles github.com vs GHES URL routing
- Prefer using the centralized GitHub error parsing (`parseGitHubError`) and `GitHubApiError` for consistent UI-friendly failures

### GitHub data loading flow
- `prsSlice` includes async thunks for PR metadata and per-resource loading:
  - `fetchPullRequest`
  - `fetchPRFiles`
  - `fetchPRComments`
  - `fetchPRReviewComments`
  - `fetchPRReviews`
- Use `loadingByResource` and `errorByResource` for UI state; legacy aggregate `isLoading` and `error` are still populated for compatibility

### Active OpenSpec context
- Active change: `openspec/changes/repository-watcher`
- Main specs synced for this change:
  - `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`
  - `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`
- Repository watcher behavior target:
  - left `PR Review` pane supports two views: controls/watchlist and repo PR list
  - `Load PR` with a PR number switches to list view with one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
  - watched repo click opens that repo list in the same pane
  - list view includes Back action to return to controls/watchlist

### Modifying dummy data
- Edit [`src/services/dummyData.ts`](src/services/dummyData.ts)
- The `dummyPRContext` export is used by `ChatWindow` and `Sidebar` in demo mode

## Architecture Notes

### State Flow
```
User types message
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (user)
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (assistant, isStreaming: true)
  → LLMService.chatStream() yields chunks
  → ChatWindow dispatches appendStreamingContent for each chunk
  → ChatWindow dispatches finalizeStreamingMessage when done
```

### Config Priority
```
localStorage override > environment variable > hardcoded default
```

### GHES URL Construction
```typescript
// github.com → https://api.github.com
// mycompany.com → https://mycompany.com/api/v3
```

## Known Limitations / Future Work

- No pagination for PR lists
- No OAuth flow (PAT only)
- No conversation persistence across page reloads

## Configuration

### Environment Variables (with defaults)
```
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=     # "github.com" or URL for GHES
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=         # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=        # API endpoint URL
VITE_LLM_MODEL=           # Model name
VITE_DEMO_MODE=           # "true" or "false"
```

### Runtime Override
Settings dialog allows overriding env vars at runtime (persisted to localStorage).

---

## Architecture

### Services
```
src/services/
├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)
├── llm.ts         # Unified interface for OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM Proxy
└── dummyData.ts   # Sample PR data for demo mode
```

### Redux Store
```
src/store/
├── index.ts              # Store configuration
├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
└── slices/
    ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
    ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
    └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
```

### Components
```
src/components/
├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
└── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
```

---

## Data Types

### GitHub PR
```typescript
interface PullRequest {
  id: number;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  body: string | null;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  merged: boolean;
  merged_at: string | null;
  user: GitHubUser;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
  base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
  url: string;
  html_url: string;
  diff_url: string;
  additions: number;
  deletions: number;
  changed_files: number;
  comments: number;
  review_comments: number;
  commits: number;
  labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
  requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
}
```

### Chat Message
```typescript
interface ChatMessage {
  id: string;
  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isStreaming?: boolean;
  error?: string;
}
```

### App Config
```typescript
interface AppConfig {
  githubPat: string;
  llmApiKey: string;
  githubInstance: string;
  llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
  llmEndpoint: string;
  llmModel: string;
  demoMode: boolean;
}
```

---

## Error Handling

### LLM API Errors
- Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
- Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
- Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
- Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message

### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
- Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
- Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
- Not found (404): Repository/PR not found

---

### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)

Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.

#### Quality
- [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
- [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
- [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.

#### UX
- [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
  ```ts
  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
    () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
      || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
  );
  ```
  Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
- [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /home/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/.github/workflows/docker-release.yml
+name: Docker Release
+
+on:
+  release:
+    types:
+      - published
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  packages: write
+
+jobs:
+  publish:
+    name: Build and Publish Docker Image
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+
+      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
+        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
+
+      - name: Log in to GHCR
+        uses: docker/login-action@v3
+        with:
+          registry: ghcr.io
+          username: ${{ github.actor }}
+          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+      - name: Compute Docker tags
+        id: version
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+
+          image="ghcr.io/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY,,}"
+          last_semver_tag="$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --match 'v[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*' 2>/dev/null || true)"
+
+          if [ -n "$last_semver_tag" ]; then
+            distance="$(git rev-list "${last_semver_tag}..HEAD" --count)"
+            base_tag="$last_semver_tag"
+          else
+            distance="$(git rev-list HEAD --count)"
+            base_tag="v0.0.0"
+          fi
+
+          version_tag="${base_tag}-${distance}"
+          sha_tag="sha-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
+
+          echo "image=$image" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          echo "version_tag=$version_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          echo "sha_tag=$sha_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Build and push image
+        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
+        with:
+          context: .
+          file: ./Dockerfile
+          push: true
+          tags: |
+            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}
+            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.sha_tag }}
+          labels: |
+            org.opencontainers.image.source=${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}
+            org.opencontainers.image.revision=${{ github.sha }}
+            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}
+
*** End Patch

name: Docker Release

on:
  release:
    types:
      - published

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Build and Publish Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Compute Docker tags
        id: version
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          image="ghcr.io/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY,,}"
          last_semver_tag="$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --match 'v[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*' 2>/dev/null || true)"

          if [ -n "$last_semver_tag" ]; then
            distance="$(git rev-list "${last_semver_tag}..HEAD" --count)"
            base_tag="$last_semver_tag"
          else
            distance="$(git rev-list HEAD --count)"
            base_tag="v0.0.0"
          fi

          version_tag="${base_tag}-${distance}"
          sha_tag="sha-${GITHUB_SHA::7}"

          echo "image=$image" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_tag=$version_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "sha_tag=$sha_tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Build and push image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          file: ./Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: |
            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}
            ${{ steps.version.outputs.image }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.sha_tag }}
          labels: |
            org.opencontainers.image.source=${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}
            org.opencontainers.image.revision=${{ github.sha }}
            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ steps.version.outputs.version_tag }}

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/README.md
@@
 ## Running the App
@@
 ### Production Build
@@
 # Or with Python
 python3 -m http.server 8080 --directory dist
 ```
+
+### Docker (Local)
+
+Build and run the production image locally:
+
+```bash
+npm run docker:build
+npm run docker:run
+```
+
+Or run the equivalent Docker commands directly:
+
+```bash
+docker build -t pr-review-chatbot:local .
+docker run --rm -p 8080:80 pr-review-chatbot:local
+```
+
+Open [http://localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080).
+
+### Docker (GHCR)
+
+Pull and run a published release image:
+
+```bash
+docker pull ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>:vX.Y.Z-N
+docker run --rm -p 8080:80 ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>:vX.Y.Z-N
+```
+
+Tag format for release images:
+
+- `vX.Y.Z-N` where:
+  - `vX.Y.Z` is the most recent semver tag reachable from `HEAD`
+  - `N` is commit distance from that tag
+- Fallback when no semver tag exists: `v0.0.0-N`
+- Additional trace tag: `sha-<shortsha>`
+
+Images are published by GitHub Actions when a GitHub Release is published.
 
 ---
@@
 ## Troubleshooting
@@
 ### CORS errors with LiteLLM
 If you see CORS errors when using a local LiteLLM proxy, add CORS headers to your proxy configuration or use a browser extension to disable CORS for development.
+
+---
+
+## Distribution Options
+
+- **Docker + GHCR (current):** Best for consistent cross-platform runs in CI, servers, and developer machines.
+- **Tauri desktop app (alternative):** Good next step if you want native installers for macOS/Linux/Windows with a smaller runtime footprint than Electron.
 
 ---
*** End Patch
